visits.note: Patient P012 is a 55-year-old male with history of Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. He presents with symptoms of fatigue, weight loss, and increased thirst. On examination, his BP is 140/90, HbA1c is 7.2, and eGFR is 72. He is started on Metformin 500mg twice daily and Lisinopril 10mg daily.

Patient P012 returns 8 months later with worsening symptoms of fatigue and weight loss. His BP is 150/95, HbA1c is 8.1, and eGFR is 60. He is started on Atorvastatin 40mg daily to control his cholesterol. His Lisinopril is also increased to 20mg daily.

Patient P012 returns 6 months later with worsening symptoms of fatigue, weight loss, and swelling in legs. His BP is 160/100, HbA1c is 9.0, and eGFR is 45. He is started on SGLT2 inhibitor, Empagliflozin 25mg daily, to improve his kidney function. His Lisinopril is increased to 30mg daily.
